title: hemizonia fasciculata subsp. lobbii
intro: Hemizonia fasciculata subsp. lobbii, also known as Lob's tarweed, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its yellow flower clusters and aromatic foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lob's tarweed include relief from skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Hemizonia fasciculata subsp. lobbii can be prepared as a topical salve or infused oil, and is also used in teas and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
